ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990年（平成2年）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975年（昭和50年）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[ja:湯田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[sn:uta Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Sue ware" to "Sue pottery"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|deity}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}}. On {{ill|August 28 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月28日}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|ja|八柱神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:湯田神社]] == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008年（平成20年）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929年（昭和4年）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983年（昭和58年）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991年（平成3年）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990年（平成2年）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975年（昭和50年）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[ja:湯田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[sn:uta Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990年（平成2年）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975年（昭和50年）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[ja:湯田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[sn:uta Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|deity}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}}. On {{ill|August 28 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月28日}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|ja|八柱神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:湯田神社]] == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008年（平成20年）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929年（昭和4年）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983年（昭和58年）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991年（平成3年）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990年（平成2年）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975年（昭和50年）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[ja:湯田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[sn:uta Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990年（平成2年）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975年（昭和50年）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[ja:湯田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[sn:uta Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|deity}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}}. On {{ill|August 28 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月28日}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|ja|八柱神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:湯田神社]] == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008年（平成20年）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929年（昭和4年）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983年（昭和58年）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991年（平成3年）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990年（平成2年）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975年（昭和50年）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[ja:湯田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[sn:uta Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990年（平成2年）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975年（昭和50年）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[ja:湯田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[sn:uta Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|deity}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}}. On {{ill|August 28 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月28日}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|ja|八柱神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:湯田神社]] == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008年（平成20年）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929年（昭和4年）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983年（昭和58年）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991年（平成3年）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990年（平成2年）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975年（昭和50年）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7== See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[ja:湯田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[sn:uta Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990年（平成2年）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975年（昭和50年）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7== See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[ja:湯田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[sn:uta Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|deity}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}}. On {{ill|August 28 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月28日}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|ja|八柱神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:湯田神社]] == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008年（平成20年）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929年（昭和4年）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983年（昭和58年）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991年（平成3年）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990年（平成2年）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975年（昭和50年）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also! == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[ja:湯田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[sn:uta Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990年（平成2年）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975年（昭和50年）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[ja:湯田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[sn:uta Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "1年" to "1st year "
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|deity}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}}. On {{ill|August 28 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月28日}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|ja|八柱神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:湯田神社]] == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008年（平成20年）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929年（昭和4年）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983年（昭和58年）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3年）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990年（平成2年）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975年（昭和50年）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[ja:湯田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[sn:uta Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990年（平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975年（昭和50年）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[ja:湯田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[sn:uta Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|deity}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}}. On {{ill|August 28 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月28日}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|ja|八柱神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[ja:湯田神社]] == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008年（平成20年）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929年（昭和4年）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58年）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990年（平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975年（昭和50年）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[ja:湯田神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:湯田神社]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[sn:uta Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuta Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]
