ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|産土神|" to "|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神|12=draft}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=draft}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|12=draft}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|12=draft}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=draft}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=draft}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|12=draft}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=draft}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|12=draft}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日|12=draft}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=draft}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|12=draft}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|deity|12=draft}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|12=draft}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神|12=draft}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|12=draft}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|12=draft}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|12=draft}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺|12=draft}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|12=draft}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永|12=draft}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|12=draft}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明|12=draft}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft}}. On {{ill|August 28 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月28日|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|12=draft}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008th year （平成20th year ）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929th year （昭和4th year ）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "rank" to "Rank"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|12=draft|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft|12=draft}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|12=draft|12=draft}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=draft|12=draft}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|12=draft|12=draft}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=draft|12=draft}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft|12=draft}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日|12=draft|12=draft}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=draft|12=draft}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|deity|12=draft|12=draft}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|12=draft|12=draft}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神|12=draft|12=draft}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|12=draft|12=draft}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|12=draft|12=draft}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft|12=draft}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺|12=draft|12=draft}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. On {{ill|August 28 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月28日|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft|12=draft}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|12=draft|12=draft}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008th year （平成20th year ）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929th year （昭和4th year ）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}

Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008th year （平成20th year ）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929th year （昭和4th year ）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|明治|" to "|明治|1=Meiji era|"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|12=draft|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft|12=draft}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|12=draft|12=draft}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=draft|12=draft}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|12=draft|12=draft}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=draft|12=draft}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft|12=draft}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日|12=draft|12=draft}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=draft|12=draft}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|deity|12=draft|12=draft}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|12=draft|12=draft}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神|12=draft|12=draft}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|12=draft|12=draft}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|12=draft|12=draft}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft|12=draft}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺|12=draft|12=draft}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. On {{ill|August 28 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月28日|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft|12=draft}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|12=draft|12=draft}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008th year （平成20th year ）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929th year （昭和4th year ）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}

Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft|12=draft}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|12=draft|12=draft}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008th year （平成20th year ）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929th year （昭和4th year ）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] to [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神|12=draft|12=draft}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|12=draft|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|12=draft|12=draft}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=draft|12=draft}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|12=draft|12=draft}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=draft|12=draft}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|12=draft|12=draft}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|12=draft|12=draft}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=draft|12=draft}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=draft|12=draft}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft|12=draft}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|12=draft|12=draft}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日|12=draft|12=draft}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=draft|12=draft}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|deity|12=draft|12=draft}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|12=draft|12=draft}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神|12=draft|12=draft}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|12=draft|12=draft}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|12=draft|12=draft}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|12=draft|12=draft}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺|12=draft|12=draft}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|12=draft|12=draft}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. On {{ill|August 28 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月28日|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft|12=draft}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|12=draft|12=draft}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008th year （平成20th year ）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929th year （昭和4th year ）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}

In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|12=draft|12=draft}}. On {{ill|August 28 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月28日|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|12=draft|12=draft}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|12=draft|12=draft}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008th year （平成20th year ）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929th year （昭和4th year ）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|12=draft" to "|qq=draft"
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|qq=draft|qq=draft}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On {{ill|August 28 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008th year （平成20th year ）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929th year （昭和4th year ）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}}

revision.comment: 12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:湯田神社 up to rev 77002068
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 湯田神社 |画像= [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]小俣町湯田[[小字|字]]孤山983 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 30 | 緯度秒 = 14.3 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 38 | 経度秒 = 57.4 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[神大市比売|大歳御祖命]]
御前神 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
[[皇大神宮]]摂社 |創建= 伝・[[雄略天皇]]朝 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]] }} '''湯田神社'''（ゆたじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮]]（内宮）の[[摂末社|摂社]]。内宮の摂社27社のうち第9位である宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：9 - 11ページ。[[農耕]]の[[守護神]]を祀る[[神社]]である伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：65ページ。 == 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]小俣町湯田[[小字|字]]孤山983辰巳出版（2013）：43ページ、湯田[[集落]]の南西端に鎮座する式内社研究会 編（1990）：234ページ。鎮座地「湯田」の[[地名]]は[[神田]]を意味する[[斎田]]（ゆた）に由来するとされ、神社周辺は伊勢神宮の神田であったと考えられ、[[境内]]を水田に囲まれている。神田を開拓したのは内宮の[[神主]]であった[[荒木田氏]]である学研パブリッシング（2013）：65ページ。 社地の[[面積]]は463坪（約1,530.6[[平方メートル|m2]]）式内社研究会 編（1990）：235ページ。[[社殿]]は他の神宮摂末社よりも大きめに造られている。社殿は[[神明造]]の[[板葺]]で南向きに建っており、一重の[[玉垣]]に囲まれ、1基の[[鳥居]]を備える。[[神体]]はない金子（1983）：59ページ。 毎年[[12月12日]]に「摂社[[庚申待|日待]]」という住民が湯田神社に参拝する習慣が残されている。こうした[[世俗]]的な[[信仰]]を集める神宮所管の神社には、[[子安神社 (伊勢市)|子安神社]]・[[園相神社]]・[[堅田神社]]・[[赤崎神社]]が挙げられる櫻井（1991）：294ページ。 == 祭神 == [[祭神]]は、[[神大市比売|大歳御祖命]]（おおとしのみおやのみこと）と御前神（みまえのかみ）。両神とも[[地域]]の農耕の[[神 (神道)|神]]とされる。大歳御祖命は[[年神|大歳神]]の母である神大市比売命（かむおおいちひめのみこと）を指す。御前神ではなく鳴宸電（なるいかつち）を祀るという説もある。 [[古代]]には長さ4.5尺（≒1.36m）×広さ4尺（≒1.21m）×高さ3尺（≒90.9[[センチメートル|cm]]）の社殿を2つ有した福山ほか（1975）：150ページが、[[江戸時代]]に復興した際に片方の社殿は中絶し、1つの社殿に2柱の神が同座する宇治山田市役所 編（1929）：10ページ。 == 歴史 == [[雄略天皇]]の治世に創建されたとされ、古代には造神宮使が造り替えを行う6社のうちの1社とされた。しかし中世になると建て替えは不安定となり、[[文永]]4年（[[1267年]]）には社頭に材木が用意されたものの建て替えられず、後の時代には地元有力者の手で建て替えられるようになり、[[文明 (日本)|文明]]3年（[[1471年]]）には[[役夫工米]]をもって建て替えを命じたことが記されたのを最後に、造り替えの記録は途絶えた。その後は地域の[[産土神]]として祀られるようになったようである。 [[寛文]]3年[[8月28日 (旧暦)|8月28日]]（[[グレゴリオ暦]]：[[1663年]][[9月29日]]）に大宮司・[[河辺精長|河邊精長]]（大中臣精長）は当時「湯田社」と呼ばれていた産土神の神社の境内に湯田神社を再興した式内社研究会 編（1990）：234 - 235ページ。[[1889年]]（明治22年）に隣接する八柱神社に産土神が合祀されるまでそのまま産土神と神宮摂社が並立する時代が続き、八柱神社は[[1910年]]（明治43年）に有田神社に合祀され、跡地は竹林となった。 [[1923年]]（大正12年）3月に建て替えられ、[[1981年]]（昭和56年）5月に大修繕が行われた。 == 交通 == 鎮座地周辺は[[土師器]]・[[須恵器]]などが出土する[[遺跡]]や[[古墳]]群が数多く、湯田神社の創建と発展に関連があると考えられる。 * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]][[伊勢市駅]]より[[三重交通]][[路線バス|バス]]早馬瀬口行きに約15分乗車、「湯田」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、徒歩約8分（約400m）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：51ページ。[[三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線]]より約200m入ったところにある。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[玉城インターチェンジ|玉城IC]]より約14分（約7.0[[キロメートル|km]]）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008年（平成20年）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929年（昭和4年）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983年（昭和58年）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991年（平成3年）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[皇學館大学]]出版部、1990年（平成2年）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975年（昭和50年）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013年11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]] * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:ゆたしんしや}} [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]]

revision.comment: Bot: restore translated version
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|qq=draft|qq=draft}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|deity|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On {{ill|August 28 (lunar calendar)|ja|8月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008th year （平成20th year ）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929th year （昭和4th year ）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:湯田神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yuta Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|湯田神社|version=77002068|comment=Imported full ja history}}
