{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae-no-Kami|qq=}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agriculture|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda|qq=}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/settlement|qq=}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|qq=}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precinct|qq=}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsubo|qq=}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|qq=}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred object|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintai|qq=}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 12|qq=}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deities|qq=}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae no Kami|qq=}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agricultural deities|qq=}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naru Ikazuchi|qq=}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient times|qq=}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaku|qq=}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōjingūshi|qq=}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medieval period|qq=}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei|qq=}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corvée rice|qq=}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunmei|qq=}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}}. On {{ill|August 28 (Lunisolar)|ja|8月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 28 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arita Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-shi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangū Line|qq=}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamaseguchi|qq=}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008th year （平成20th year ）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929th year （昭和4th year ）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|湯田神社|version=77002068|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Deity]] → [[Kami]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae-no-Kami|qq=}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agriculture|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda|qq=}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/settlement|qq=}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|qq=}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precinct|qq=}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsubo|qq=}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|qq=}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred object|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintai|qq=}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 12|qq=}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deities|qq=}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae no Kami|qq=}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agricultural deities|qq=}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naru Ikazuchi|qq=}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient times|qq=}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaku|qq=}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōjingūshi|qq=}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medieval period|qq=}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei|qq=}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corvée rice|qq=}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunmei|qq=}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}}. On {{ill|August 28 (Lunisolar)|ja|8月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 28 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arita Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-shi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangū Line|qq=}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamaseguchi|qq=}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008th year （平成20th year ）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929th year （昭和4th year ）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|湯田神社|version=77002068|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Inner Shrine]] → [[Kōtai Jingū]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae-no-Kami|qq=}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agriculture|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda|qq=}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/settlement|qq=}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|qq=}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precinct|qq=}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsubo|qq=}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|qq=}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred object|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintai|qq=}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 12|qq=}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|enshrined deities|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deities|qq=}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae no Kami|qq=}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agricultural deities|qq=}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naru Ikazuchi|qq=}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient times|qq=}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaku|qq=}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōjingūshi|qq=}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medieval period|qq=}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei|qq=}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corvée rice|qq=}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunmei|qq=}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}}. On {{ill|August 28 (Lunisolar)|ja|8月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 28 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arita Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-shi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangū Line|qq=}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamaseguchi|qq=}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008th year （平成20th year ）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929th year （昭和4th year ）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|湯田神社|version=77002068|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae-no-Kami|qq=}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}} | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agriculture|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Ise|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise|qq=}} Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda|qq=}} {{ill|settlement|ja|集落|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/settlement|qq=}}Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|qq=}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precinct|qq=}} covers 463 {{ill|tsubo|ja|坪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tsubo|qq=}} (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei-zukuri|qq=}} style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|qq=}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}} fence, and features one {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}}. There is no physical {{ill|sacred object|ja|神体|1=Shintai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred object|13=User:Immanuelle/Shintai|qq=}} enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on {{ill|December 12|ja|12月12日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 12|qq=}}, there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deities|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deities|qq=}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae no Kami|qq=}}. Both are considered {{ill|agricultural deities|ja|神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agricultural deities|qq=}} of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naru Ikazuchi|qq=}} is worshipped. In {{ill|ancient times|ja|古代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient times|qq=}}, there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 {{ill|shaku|ja|尺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaku|qq=}} (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Yūryaku|qq=}}, and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōjingūshi|qq=}}. However, in the {{ill|medieval period|ja|中世|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medieval period|qq=}}, reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in {{ill|Bunei|ja|文永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei|qq=}} 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corvée rice|qq=}} in {{ill|Bunmei|ja|文明|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunmei|qq=}} 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神|1=Ubusunagami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubusunagami|qq=}}. On {{ill|August 28 (Lunisolar)|ja|8月28日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 28 (lunar calendar)|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbun|qq=}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arita Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, as well as {{ill|ancient tombs|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ancient tombs|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}}, which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-shi Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangū Line|qq=}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}) and {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Yamada Line|qq=}}, take a {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamaseguchi|qq=}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008th year （平成20th year ）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929th year （昭和4th year ）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|湯田神社|version=77002068|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]]

{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae-no-Kami|qq=}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agriculture|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda|qq=}} [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]]Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|qq=}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precinct|qq=}} covers 463 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|qq=}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence, and features one [[:en:Torii|torii]]. There is no physical [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on [[:en:December 12|December 12]], there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deities|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deities|qq=}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae no Kami|qq=}}. Both are considered [[:en:Deity|agricultural deities]] of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naru Ikazuchi|qq=}} is worshipped. In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient times]], there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]] (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōjingūshi|qq=}}. However, in the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corvée rice|qq=}} in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. On [[:en:August 28|August 28 (Lunisolar)]], [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arita Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], as well as [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]], which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}) and [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamaseguchi|qq=}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008th year （平成20th year ）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929th year （昭和4th year ）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|湯田神社|version=77002068|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = [[File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg|280px]] | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|34|30|14.3|N|136|38|57.4|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Mimae-no-Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae-no-Kami|qq=}} | type = [[Shikinaisha]] (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) }} '''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11. The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agriculture|qq=}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65. == Overview == [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda|qq=}} [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]]Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|qq=}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65. The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precinct|qq=}} covers 463 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,530.6 m²)Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|qq=}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū. The building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|qq=}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence, and features one [[:en:Torii|torii]]. There is no physical [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] enshrinedKaneko (1983): p. 59. Each year on [[:en:December 12|December 12]], there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}Sakurai (1991): p. 294. == Deities == The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deities|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deities|qq=}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|qq=}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae no Kami|qq=}}. Both are considered [[:en:Deity|agricultural deities]] of the local region. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naru Ikazuchi|qq=}} is worshipped. In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient times]], there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]] (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) highFukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150. However, after being restored in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], one of the buildings was discontinued, and the two deities now share a single shrine buildingUjiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10. == History == The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōjingūshi|qq=}}. However, in the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corvée rice|qq=}} in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 3 (1471). After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. On [[:en:August 28|August 28 (Lunisolar)]], [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|qq=}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arita Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove. The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 (1923) and underwent major renovations in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 56 (1981). == Access == The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], as well as [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]], which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development. * From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|JR東海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}) and [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamaseguchi|qq=}} for about 15 minutes. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|qq=}}. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]]. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、2008th year （平成20th year ）12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、1929th year （昭和4th year ）1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、1983rd year （昭和58th year ）8月10日、276p. * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、1991st year （平成3rd year ）11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、1990th year （平成2nd year ）2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[有田村 (三重県)]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_03.jpg 湯田神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m05/0501.html 湯田神社（ゆたじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|湯田神社|version=77002068|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:湯田神社]] [[sn:Yuta Shrine]] [[en:Yuta Shrine]] [[sn:uta Shrine]]
