<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17225818]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.503972|N|136.649278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref> The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agriculture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=farming|WD=Q24886094}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65.</ref>
== Overview ==
[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43</ref>, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]]<ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234</ref>. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields<ref name="ic"/>. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65</ref>.
The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precinct|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}} covers 463 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,530.6 m²)<ref name="sk5">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235</ref>. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū<ref name="ic"/>. The building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杮葺|1=wood shingle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q199388}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence, and features one [[:en:Torii|torii]]<ref name="sk5"/>. There is no physical [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] enshrined<ref>Kaneko (1983): p. 59</ref>.
Each year on [[:en:December 12|December 12]], there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine<ref name="sk5"/>. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1991): p. 294</ref>.
== Deities ==
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deities|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>. Both are considered [[:en:Deity|agricultural deities]] of the local region<ref name="ic"/><ref name="tm"/>. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神大市比売|1=Kamuo Ichihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588867}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}<ref name="tm"/>. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naru Ikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is worshipped<ref name="tm"/>.
In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient times]], there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]] (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) high<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref>. However, after being restored in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], one of the buildings was discontinued<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, and the two deities now share a single shrine building<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10</ref>.
== History ==
The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōjingūshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="sk4"/>. However, in the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corvée rice|1=corvée rice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488146}} in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 3 (1471)<ref name="sk4"/>. After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]<ref name="sk4"/>.
On [[:en:August 28|August 28 (Lunisolar)]], [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"<ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235</ref>. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arita Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove<ref name="sk5"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 (1923)<ref name="uy"/> and underwent major renovations in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 56 (1981)<ref name="sk5"/>.
== Access ==
The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], as well as [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]], which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development<ref name="sk5"/>.
* From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]) and [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamaseguchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for about 15 minutes<ref name="tm"/>. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)<ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51</ref>. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}<ref name="sk4"/>. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17225818]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.503972|N|136.649278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref> The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agriculture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=farming|WD=Q24886094}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65.</ref>
== Overview ==
[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43</ref>, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]]<ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234</ref>. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields<ref name="ic"/>. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65</ref>.
The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precinct|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}} covers 463 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,530.6 m²)<ref name="sk5">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235</ref>. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū<ref name="ic"/>. The building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杮葺|1=wood shingle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q199388}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence, and features one [[:en:Torii|torii]]<ref name="sk5"/>. There is no physical [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] enshrined<ref>Kaneko (1983): p. 59</ref>.
Each year on [[:en:December 12|December 12]], there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine<ref name="sk5"/>. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1991): p. 294</ref>.
== Deities ==
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deities|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>. Both are considered [[:en:Deity|agricultural deities]] of the local region<ref name="ic"/><ref name="tm"/>. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神大市比売|1=Kamuo Ichihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588867}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}<ref name="tm"/>. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naru Ikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is worshipped<ref name="tm"/>.
In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient times]], there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]] (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) high<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref>. However, after being restored in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], one of the buildings was discontinued<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, and the two deities now share a single shrine building<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10</ref>.
== History ==
The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōjingūshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="sk4"/>. However, in the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corvée rice|1=corvée rice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488146}} in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 3 (1471)<ref name="sk4"/>. After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]<ref name="sk4"/>.
On [[:en:August 28|August 28 (Lunisolar)]], [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"<ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235</ref>. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arita Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove<ref name="sk5"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 (1923)<ref name="uy"/> and underwent major renovations in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 56 (1981)<ref name="sk5"/>.
== Access ==
The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], as well as [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]], which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development<ref name="sk5"/>.
* From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]) and [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamaseguchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for about 15 minutes<ref name="tm"/>. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)<ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51</ref>. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}<ref name="sk4"/>. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]].

'''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref> The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agriculture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=farming|WD=Q24886094}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65.</ref>
== Overview ==
[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43</ref>, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]]<ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234</ref>. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields<ref name="ic"/>. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65</ref>.
The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precinct|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}} covers 463 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,530.6 m²)<ref name="sk5">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235</ref>. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū<ref name="ic"/>. The building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杮葺|1=wood shingle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q199388}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence, and features one [[:en:Torii|torii]]<ref name="sk5"/>. There is no physical [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] enshrined<ref>Kaneko (1983): p. 59</ref>.
Each year on [[:en:December 12|December 12]], there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine<ref name="sk5"/>. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1991): p. 294</ref>.
== Deities ==
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deities|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>. Both are considered [[:en:Deity|agricultural deities]] of the local region<ref name="ic"/><ref name="tm"/>. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神大市比売|1=Kamuo Ichihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588867}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}<ref name="tm"/>. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naru Ikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is worshipped<ref name="tm"/>.
In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient times]], there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]] (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) high<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref>. However, after being restored in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], one of the buildings was discontinued<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, and the two deities now share a single shrine building<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10</ref>.
== History ==
The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōjingūshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="sk4"/>. However, in the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corvée rice|1=corvée rice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488146}} in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 3 (1471)<ref name="sk4"/>. After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]<ref name="sk4"/>.
On [[:en:August 28|August 28 (Lunisolar)]], [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"<ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235</ref>. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arita Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove<ref name="sk5"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 (1923)<ref name="uy"/> and underwent major renovations in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 56 (1981)<ref name="sk5"/>.
== Access ==
The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], as well as [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]], which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development<ref name="sk5"/>.
* From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]) and [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamaseguchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for about 15 minutes<ref name="tm"/>. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)<ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51</ref>. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}<ref name="sk4"/>. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17225818]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.503972|N|136.649278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref> The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agriculture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=farming|WD=Q24886094}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65.</ref>
== Overview ==
[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43</ref>, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]]<ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234</ref>. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields<ref name="ic"/>. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65</ref>.
The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precinct|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}} covers 463 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,530.6 m²)<ref name="sk5">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235</ref>. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū<ref name="ic"/>. The building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杮葺|1=wood shingle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q199388}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence, and features one [[:en:Torii|torii]]<ref name="sk5"/>. There is no physical [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] enshrined<ref>Kaneko (1983): p. 59</ref>.
Each year on [[:en:December 12|December 12]], there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine<ref name="sk5"/>. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1991): p. 294</ref>.
== Deities ==
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deities|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>. Both are considered [[:en:Deity|agricultural deities]] of the local region<ref name="ic"/><ref name="tm"/>. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神大市比売|1=Kamuo Ichihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588867}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}<ref name="tm"/>. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naru Ikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is worshipped<ref name="tm"/>.
In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient times]], there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]] (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) high<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref>. However, after being restored in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], one of the buildings was discontinued<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, and the two deities now share a single shrine building<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10</ref>.

ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[:en:小学館|小学館]]、1975th year （昭和50th year ）4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17225818]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.503972|N|136.649278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref> The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agriculture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=farming|WD=Q24886094}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65.</ref>
== Overview ==
[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43</ref>, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]]<ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234</ref>. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields<ref name="ic"/>. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65</ref>.
The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precinct|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}} covers 463 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,530.6 m²)<ref name="sk5">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235</ref>. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū<ref name="ic"/>. The building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杮葺|1=wood shingle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q199388}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence, and features one [[:en:Torii|torii]]<ref name="sk5"/>. There is no physical [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] enshrined<ref>Kaneko (1983): p. 59</ref>.
Each year on [[:en:December 12|December 12]], there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine<ref name="sk5"/>. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1991): p. 294</ref>.
== Deities ==
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deities|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>. Both are considered [[:en:Deity|agricultural deities]] of the local region<ref name="ic"/><ref name="tm"/>. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神大市比売|1=Kamuo Ichihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588867}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}<ref name="tm"/>. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naru Ikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is worshipped<ref name="tm"/>.
In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient times]], there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]] (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) high<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref>. However, after being restored in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], one of the buildings was discontinued<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, and the two deities now share a single shrine building<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10</ref>.
== History ==
The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōjingūshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="sk4"/>. However, in the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corvée rice|1=corvée rice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488146}} in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 3 (1471)<ref name="sk4"/>. After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]<ref name="sk4"/>.
On [[:en:August 28|August 28 (Lunisolar)]], [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"<ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235</ref>. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arita Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove<ref name="sk5"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 (1923)<ref name="uy"/> and underwent major renovations in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 56 (1981)<ref name="sk5"/>.
== Access ==
The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], as well as [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]], which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development<ref name="sk5"/>.
* From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]) and [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamaseguchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for about 15 minutes<ref name="tm"/>. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)<ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51</ref>. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}<ref name="sk4"/>. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]].

It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref> The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agriculture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=farming|WD=Q24886094}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65.</ref>
== Overview ==
[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43</ref>, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]]<ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234</ref>. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields<ref name="ic"/>. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65</ref>.

The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū<ref name="ic"/>. The building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杮葺|1=wood shingle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q199388}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence, and features one [[:en:Torii|torii]]<ref name="sk5"/>. There is no physical [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] enshrined<ref>Kaneko (1983): p. 59</ref>.
Each year on [[:en:December 12|December 12]], there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine<ref name="sk5"/>. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1991): p. 294</ref>.
== Deities ==
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deities|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>. Both are considered [[:en:Deity|agricultural deities]] of the local region<ref name="ic"/><ref name="tm"/>. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神大市比売|1=Kamuo Ichihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588867}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}<ref name="tm"/>. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naru Ikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is worshipped<ref name="tm"/>.
In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient times]], there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]] (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) high<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref>. However, after being restored in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], one of the buildings was discontinued<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, and the two deities now share a single shrine building<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10</ref>.
== History ==
The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōjingūshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="sk4"/>. However, in the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corvée rice|1=corvée rice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488146}} in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 3 (1471)<ref name="sk4"/>. After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]<ref name="sk4"/>.
On [[:en:August 28|August 28 (Lunisolar)]], [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"<ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235</ref>. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arita Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove<ref name="sk5"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 (1923)<ref name="uy"/> and underwent major renovations in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 56 (1981)<ref name="sk5"/>.
== Access ==
The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], as well as [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]], which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development<ref name="sk5"/>.
* From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]) and [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamaseguchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for about 15 minutes<ref name="tm"/>. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)<ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51</ref>. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}<ref name="sk4"/>. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17225818]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.503972|N|136.649278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref> The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agriculture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=farming|WD=Q24886094}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65.</ref>
== Overview ==
[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43</ref>, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]]<ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234</ref>. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields<ref name="ic"/>. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65</ref>.
The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precinct|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}} covers 463 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,530.6 m²)<ref name="sk5">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235</ref>. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū<ref name="ic"/>. The building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杮葺|1=wood shingle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q199388}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence, and features one [[:en:Torii|torii]]<ref name="sk5"/>. There is no physical [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] enshrined<ref>Kaneko (1983): p. 59</ref>.
Each year on [[:en:December 12|December 12]], there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine<ref name="sk5"/>. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1991): p. 294</ref>.
== Deities ==
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deities|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>. (1975): p. 150</ref>. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:湯田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17225818]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = {{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Yuta-jinja 01.jpg | location = Kodoyama 983, Yuta, Obata-cho, [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.503972|N|136.649278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = {{ill|Kamuo Ichihime|WD=Q11588867}} | type = {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Traditionally during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]] | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | location = {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''{{ill|Yuta Shrine|ja|湯田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuta Shrine|qq=}}''' (*Yuta Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in [[Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan. It Ranks ninth among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.<ref>Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp. 9–11.</ref> The shrine enshrines deities associated with the protection of {{ill|agriculture|ja|農耕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/agriculture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=farming|WD=Q24886094}}.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 65.</ref>
== Overview ==
[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43</ref>, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]]<ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234</ref>. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields<ref name="ic"/>. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65</ref>.
The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precinct|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}} covers 463 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,530.6 m²)<ref name="sk5">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235</ref>. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū<ref name="ic"/>. The building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杮葺|1=wood shingle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q199388}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence, and features one [[:en:Torii|torii]]<ref name="sk5"/>. There is no physical [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] enshrined<ref>Kaneko (1983): p. 59</ref>.
Each year on [[:en:December 12|December 12]], there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine<ref name="sk5"/>. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1991): p. 294</ref>.
== Deities ==
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deities|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>. Both are considered [[:en:Deity|agricultural deities]] of the local region<ref name="ic"/><ref name="tm"/>. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神大市比売|1=Kamuo Ichihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588867}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}<ref name="tm"/>. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naru Ikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is worshipped<ref name="tm"/>.
In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient times]], there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]] (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) high<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref>. However, after being restored in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], one of the buildings was discontinued<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, and the two deities now share a single shrine building<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10</ref>.
== History ==
The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōjingūshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="sk4"/>. However, in the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corvée rice|1=corvée rice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488146}} in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 3 (1471)<ref name="sk4"/>. After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]<ref name="sk4"/>.
On [[:en:August 28|August 28 (Lunisolar)]], [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"<ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235</ref>. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arita Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove<ref name="sk5"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 (1923)<ref name="uy"/> and underwent major renovations in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 56 (1981)<ref name="sk5"/>.
== Access ==
The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], as well as [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]], which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development<ref name="sk5"/>.
* From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]) and [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamaseguchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for about 15 minutes<ref name="tm"/>. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)<ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51</ref>. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}<ref name="sk4"/>. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]].

== Overview ==
[[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]][[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]] Omata-cho, Yuda, Aza Kozan 983<ref name="tm">Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 43</ref>, located at the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Yuda|ja|湯田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuda|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]]<ref name="sk4">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 234</ref>. The {{ill|place name|ja|地名|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/place name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=toponym|WD=Q7884789}} "Yuda" is believed to derive from "Yuta," meaning {{ill|sacred rice field|ja|斎田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sacred rice field|qq=}}, indicating that the area around the shrine was once sacred rice fields for Ise Jingū, surrounded by paddy fields<ref name="ic"/>. These sacred fields were developed by the {{ill|Arakida clan|ja|荒木田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida clan|qq=}}, who served as priests of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}}<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 65</ref>.
The {{ill|shrine precinct|ja|社地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine precinct|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 寺社領|1=jisha-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11458058}} covers 463 [[:en:Pyeong|tsubo]] (approximately 1,530.6 m²)<ref name="sk5">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 235</ref>. The {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is larger than those of other auxiliary shrines of Ise Jingū<ref name="ic"/>. The building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with {{ill|board roofing|ja|板葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/board roofing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 杮葺|1=wood shingle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q199388}}, facing south, surrounded by a single-layer [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] fence, and features one [[:en:Torii|torii]]<ref name="sk5"/>. There is no physical [[:en:Shintai|Shintai]] enshrined<ref>Kaneko (1983): p. 59</ref>.
Each year on [[:en:December 12|December 12]], there is a tradition called "Auxiliary Shrine {{ill|Kōshin-machi|ja|庚申待|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōshin-machi|qq=}}," where locals visit Yuda Shrine<ref name="sk5"/>. Other shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Jingū that attract such secular faith include {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社_(伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, {{ill|Sonai Shrine|ja|園相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonai Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Akasaki Shrine|ja|赤崎神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akasaki Shrine|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1991): p. 294</ref>.
== Deities ==
The {{ill|Saijin|lt=Saijin|lt=Enshrined deities|ja|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/enshrined deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミ}} are {{ill|Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja|大歳御祖命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mimae no Kami|ja|御前神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimae no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/>. Both are considered [[:en:Deity|agricultural deities]] of the local region<ref name="ic"/><ref name="tm"/>. Ōtoshi no Mioya no Mikoto refers to {{ill|Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja|神大市比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Ōichi-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神大市比売|1=Kamuo Ichihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588867}}, the mother of {{ill|Toshigami|ja|大歳神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshigami|qq=}}<ref name="tm"/>. Some theories suggest that instead of Mimae no Kami, {{ill|Naru Ikazuchi|ja|鳴宸電|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naru Ikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is worshipped<ref name="tm"/>.
In [[:en:Ancient history|ancient times]], there were two shrine buildings measuring 4.5 [[:en:Shaku (unit)|shaku]] (approx. 1.36 m) long, 4 shaku (approx. 1.21 m) wide, and 3 shaku (approx. 90.9 cm) high<ref name="jg">Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150</ref>. However, after being restored in the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], one of the buildings was discontinued<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>, and the two deities now share a single shrine building<ref name="uy">Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10</ref>.
== History ==
The shrine is believed to have been established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]], and in ancient times, it was one of the six shrines whose reconstruction was overseen by the {{ill|Zōjingūshi|ja|造神宮使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōjingūshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="sk4"/>. However, in the [[:en:Middle Ages|medieval period]], reconstruction became irregular, with materials prepared for rebuilding in [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei]] 4 (1267) but the work left unfinished. In later periods, reconstruction was carried out by local leaders, with the last recorded rebuilding ordered using {{ill|corvée rice|ja|役夫工米|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corvée rice|1=corvée rice|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488146}} in [[:en:Civilization|Bunmei]] 3 (1471)<ref name="sk4"/>. After that, records of reconstruction ceased, and it seems the shrine was revered as a local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]<ref name="sk4"/>.
On [[:en:August 28|August 28 (Lunisolar)]], [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] 3 (Gregorian calendar: September 29, 1663), the shrine was revived by {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河辺精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|1=Kiyonaga Kawabe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q92924151}} (also known as Ōnakatomi Masanaga) within the precincts of a local Ujigami shrine then called "Yuda Shrine"<ref>Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): pp. 234–235</ref>. Until the local Ujigami was merged with {{ill|Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine|ja|八柱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yahashira Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八王子神社}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 22 (1889), both the Ujigami and the auxiliary shrine of Ise Jingū coexisted. Yahashira Shrine|lt=Yahashira Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine was later merged with {{ill|Arita Shrine|ja|有田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arita Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 43 (1910), and its former site became a bamboo grove<ref name="sk5"/>.
The shrine was rebuilt in [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 12 (1923)<ref name="uy"/> and underwent major renovations in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 56 (1981)<ref name="sk5"/>.
== Access ==
The area around the shrine is rich in archaeological sites, including relics like [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], as well as [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]], which are thought to be connected to the shrine's origin and development<ref name="sk5"/>.
* From [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]) and [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], take a [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus bound for {{ill|Hayamaseguchi|ja|早馬瀬口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayamaseguchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} for about 15 minutes<ref name="tm"/>. Get off at the "Yuda" bus stop and walk approximately 8 minutes (about 400 m)<ref name="ic2">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 51</ref>. The shrine is about 200 m from {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 37|ja|三重県道37号鳥羽松阪線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 37|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 37|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357923}}<ref name="sk4"/>. * About 14 minutes (approx. 7.0 km) by car from {{ill|Tamaki Interchange|ja|玉城インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamaki Interchange|1=Tamaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11572465}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]].
